solution:
```
class Solution:
    def longestPalindrome(self, s: str) -> str:
        def expand_around_center(s, left, right):
            while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
                left -= 1
                right += 1
            return s[left + 1:right]
        
        longest = ""
        for i in range(len(s)):
            palindrome1 = expand_around_center(s, i, i)  # odd length palindrome
            palindrome2 = expand_around_center(s, i, i + 1)  # even length palindrome
            if len(palindrome1) > len(longest):
                longest = palindrome1
            if len(palindrome2) > len(longest):
                longest = palindrome2
        return longest
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a helper function to expand around the center of a potential palindrome, checking for both odd and even length palindromes. It iterates through the string, keeping track of the longest palindrome found.
